chapter_title: About Revoking an Oath for Doing a better Action

hadith_entries.text:
971. Narrated Abdul-Rahman ibn Samorah ; 'The
Prophet said: “O Abdul-Rahman ibn Samorah! Do not seek
to be a ruler, because if you are given authority on asking for it,
then you will be held responsible for it; but if you are given it
without asking for it, then you will be helped in it (by Allah).
And whenever you take an oath to do something and later you
find that something else is better than what you intended; then
do the best action and make expiation for your oath." (Bukhari).

972. Narrated Abu-Huraira; 'The Prophet said: "He that
took an oath then he found that another action is better, let
him atone for his oath and do what he has resolutely
intended." (At-Termizi).

973. Narrated Abu-Huraira; 'The Prophet said: "By
Allah, if anyone of you insists on fulfilling an oath by which
he may harm his family, he commits a greater sin in Allahs
sight than that of dissolving his oath and making its expitation
what Allah has commanded." (Bukhari)
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
